task: Write a Python function collapse_delims(s) that splits the string on commas and semicolons, treats any run of consecutive delimiters, mixed or not, as one split, drops empty pieces at the start and end, and returns the pieces rejoined with a single comma between each. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly handles multiple delimiters, empty strings, and edge cases.
trace: First, I defined the delimiter set as a string. Then I stripped any leading or trailing whitespace from the input string and split it on the delimiters. I handled an edge case where there was a single delimiter at the end of the string by removing it if it existed. Finally, I filtered out empty pieces and rejoined the remaining ones with a comma between each. This approach ensures that run of consecutive delimiters are treated as one split and handles the named edge case.
